paradigm: action role-playing game with side-scrolling combat

danger_signals.signal: Random enemy encounters on world map
danger_signals.meaning: Shift from safe exploration to active threat state

danger_signals.visual_form: Transition to turn-based RPG menu system
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated danger requiring strategic engagement rather than reflexive combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemies visible in side-scrolling navigation spaces
danger_signals.meaning: Confined spaces increase threat proximity and limit evasion options

safety_signals.meaning: Reduced immediate threat; player controls pace of engagement
Shift to tactical system provides pause and deliberation opportunity

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Shared protagonist role in single/multiplayer modes

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Free movement without combat pressure; player initiates engagement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Side-scrolling battle arena with multiple enemies visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Real-time action combat with jumping, punching, throwing; ends when specific number of enemies defeated
Strategic turn-based system using items and special skills (shurikens, super jump, fireball)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 2D platforming sections with confined enemy encounters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Same beat'em up mechanics as side-scrolling battles but without depth axis; linear left-right movement only

environmental_storytelling.example: Confined platforming spaces signal heightened danger compared to open world map; enemies in dungeons create sense of trap or corral
Visual shift from exploration to side-scrolling combat arena communicates threat manifestation; change in visual perspective (world map to battle screen) signals state change
Boss battles using turn-based RPG menus versus standard side-scrolling beat'em up for regular enemies visually communicates enemy hierarchy and threat escalation

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Encountered on world map or in dungeons
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Engaged through side-scrolling beat'em up combat; defeated when specific kill quota reached regardless of remaining on-screen enemies
Fought in turn-based RPG style with menu-based commands; same items and skills available as regular combat but in strategic turn order
Controlled by player one; performs jumping, punching, throwing, special techniques

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Active when second player joins or in multiplayer mode
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Mirrors Jack's capabilities; shares statistics ensuring balanced power between characters

notes: Super Ninja Boy communicates world-state primarily through mechanical transitions rather than visual aesthetic differentiation. The paradigm shift from exploration (world map) to action (side-scrolling combat) to strategy (turn-based boss battles) is the primary manifestation method. Limited faction identity beyond protagonist ninja role. Environmental storytelling relies on dungeon confinement signaling danger escalation. Game uses mode switching (exploration vs. combat vs. turn-based) as primary world-state communication tool. No documented faction conflict or political allegiances in available source material. Multiplayer state (switchable between single/multiplayer) represents mechanical world-state change affecting character control but maintaining narrative equivalence.
